LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JED MCCALEB'S MOTION IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, PURSUANT TO THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE, CCP 425.16. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 2, 2015, TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 16, 2015 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT STELLAR DEVELOPMENT FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STELLAR DEVELOPMENT FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - STELLAR'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING ONE OR MORE COGNIZABLE TORT CLAIMS AGAINST STELLAR AND DOE DEFENDANTS. SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/HK

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JED MCCALEB'S MOTION TO GRANT APPLICATION TO ADMIT MARK S. PARRIS TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE is granted. The parties stipulated to the motion and no opposition has been filed. The prevailing party is to provide a formal form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DARRELL R. ATKINSON OF HOSIE/RICE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-247-6000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. TERRY GROSS & ATTY. ADAM C. BELSKY OF THE GROSS BELSKY ALONSO LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-544-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. ANDREW R. ARDINGER OF ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-773-5700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JED MCCALEB'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - McCaleb's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. The determination of the meaning of the "Agreement" attached as exhibit A to the complaint likely requires consideration of extrinsic evidence. Plaintiff's interpretation of the Agreement is well within the bounds of possible plausible interpretations and cannot be rejected as a matter of law on a non-evidentiary motion such as a demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DARRELL R. ATKINSON OF HOSIE/RICE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-247-6000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. TERRY GROSS & ATTY. ADAM C. BELSKY, TEL. NO. 1-415-544-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. ANDREW R. ARDINGER OF ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-773-5700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STELLAR DEVELOPMENT FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Stellar's demurrer to the third and fourth causes of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not alleged facts establishing his right to any particular STRs nor has he alleged that he has any property right in the nature of a stock certificate or lien for which conversion could possibly lie. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts supporting one or more cognizable tort claims against Stellar and Doe defendants. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk
